topics: 会計基礎用語/损益分岐点 (§36/§01-07)
release_blocking: F012 gross margin vs gross profit domain error

findings.issue: ZH caption uses ASCII double-quotes ("") instead of CJK brackets 「」, inconsistent with figs 1 & 2 on same page and JP source 「」
findings.current: "销售额 = 费用"→盈亏平衡点
findings.suggested: 「销售额 = 费用」→盈亏平衡点

findings.issue: EN caption fig0 uses 'Expenses' (plural) while figs 1,2 use 'Expense' (singular). JP source 費用 is mass noun - singular 'Expense' matches JP convention better; should be consistent

Section '損益分岐点' appears as §36 on p52 and §01-07 on p53 - likely two different numbering systems (textbook chapter vs curriculum sequence). Not a translation error but section_number inconsistency may confuse navigation.
findings.suggested: Verify if both numbering systems are intentional; if so, document in schema

findings.issue: Same section §01-08 title has EN inconsistency: p56='Financial Statements and Six Profits', p57='Financial Statements and the Six Profits'. Definite article difference across two pages of same section.
findings.suggested: Standardize: 'Financial Statements and the Six Types of Profit' or 'Financial Statements and Six Profits'

findings.issue: EN 'Liabilities (Other Capital)' - 'Other Capital' is literal translation artifact. Standard EN accounting term is 'Debt Capital' or simply 'Liabilities'. The parenthetical misleads; 他人資本 is a Japanese accounting category equivalent to external/borrowed capital.
findings.suggested: Debt Capital (他人資本) — or 'External Capital / Liabilities'

findings.issue: ZH '自有资本' is a direct calque from Japanese. Standard Chinese accounting term is '所有者权益' (Owners' Equity) or '股东权益'. '自有资本' is understandable but non-standard in China GAAP context; may mislead exam learners translating to Chinese financial reporting.
findings.suggested: 股东权益 / 所有者权益（自有资本）

findings.issue: ZH definition of 総資本 references '他人资本（负债）' - the parenthetical gloss of 负债 is helpful but creates inconsistency: the standalone ZH surface for 他人資本 is '他人资本（负债）' (with gloss) while here it repeats inside a definition. Acceptable but slightly redundant.
findings.current: 「他人资本（负债）」与「自有资本」的合计

findings.issue: Formula labels 総資本回転率(%) but Total Capital Turnover Ratio is conventionally dimensionless (expressed as 'times/回転', not %). Multiplying by 100 to get % is non-standard usage. JP exam may use % convention but EN/ZH translations perpetuate a potentially misleading unit label. Should note this is exam-specific convention.
findings.current: Total Capital Turnover Ratio (%) = Sales ÷ Total Capital × 100
findings.suggested: Add note: '(exam convention: expressed as %, not standard industry 回転 ratio)'

findings.issue: EN definition 'Refers to side business' for 営業外 is a simplification metaphor that may mislead. In accounting, 営業外 = non-operating items (interest income/expense, investment gains/losses) — not literally a 'side business'. The JP pedagogical metaphor works in Japanese context but 'side business' in EN implies a separate business venture.
findings.suggested: Non-operating items (e.g., interest income/expense). Note: JP pedagogy uses "side business" as a teaching metaphor only.

findings.issue: EN surface 'Profitable Bankruptcy' is a coined translation; no standard EN equivalent exists. The concept is more precisely described as 'cash-flow insolvency despite accounting profits'. Surface label is acceptable as a coined term for exam purposes but the EN definition adequately explains it.
findings.suggested: Acceptable; optionally add '(cash-flow insolvency)' as parenthetical

findings.issue: EN surface 'break-even point' (all lowercase) on p55 conflicts with 'Break-Even Point' (title case) on p52 and in glossary. Capitalization inconsistency within the same technical term.
findings.suggested: Standardize to 'Break-Even Point' (title case, matching p52 and glossary)

findings.issue: EN table row uses 'Also called gross margin' but the standalone term entity says 'Also called gross profit'. More critically: Gross Margin (%) and Gross Profit (amount) are DIFFERENT accounting concepts. JP '粗利益' = gross profit (amount), NOT gross margin (ratio/%). Using 'gross margin' here is a domain error.
findings.current: table: 'Also called gross margin' | term entity: 'Also called gross profit'
findings.suggested: Use 'gross profit' consistently. Remove 'gross margin' usage to avoid domain confusion.

findings.entity_id: table row5 税引前当期純利益
findings.issue: EN inconsistency: p61 table uses 'Pre-Tax Net Income' but p60 term entity uses 'Income Before Taxes' for same JP term 税引前当期純利益. Standard EN GAAP term is 'Income Before Taxes' or 'Pre-Tax Income'.
findings.current: p61 table: 'Pre-Tax Net Income' | p60 term: 'Income Before Taxes'

findings.issue: EN short definition 'A table that lists all of a company's assets' is factually incomplete. Balance sheet lists assets, liabilities, AND equity/net assets. Omitting liabilities and equity is a domain error that will confuse learners.
findings.current: A table that lists all of a company's assets.
findings.suggested: A statement listing a company's assets, liabilities, and net assets (equity) at a specific point in time.

findings.issue: ZH short definition '记载公司全部财产的表' (a table recording all company property) is also incomplete - balance sheet records 资产、负债 and 净资产, not just 财产 (property/assets). Same domain error as EN.
findings.current: 记载公司全部财产的表
findings.suggested: 记载公司资产、负债及净资产的财务报表
